content:
[[Shalai and Hallar]] Naya Combo Stax

Discord Server: https://discord.gg/2qvN9JpX

Tournament Performances:

Mox Masters June - 5th place in Swiss

Kaos Treasure Series 5 - 13th place in Swiss

Path to The Peak 2 - 2nd place

Path to The Peak 3 - 4th place in Swiss 

Feel free to ask any questions or leave any suggestions in the comments

INTRO:

This deck has the ability to slow the game down with some select Stax pieces, while working towards assembling 1 card + commander combos for the win. i run a fair ammount of spot removal and as much stack interaction as I can. I am leaning heavy into protection for the combo pieces.  This is because the combos are very easy to assemble,  but can be instantly stopped by any removal spell.  

WIN CONS:

1: The easiest and cleanest way to win is with [[The Red Terror]]. With this card and [[Shalai and Hallar]] on the battlefield all you will need is any form of damage from a red source to start the loop.  This can be combat damage from your commander or [[The Red Terror]].  You can also use any other red creture in combat.

2: Next would be [[Heliod, Sun-Crowned]]. With this card and your commander out you can pay [[symbol:w]][[symbol:1]] and give Shalai and Hallar Lifelink. Then move to combat and attack with [[Shalai and Hallar]].  This combat damage will trigger [[Heliod, Sun Crowned]]. A +1 /+1 counter will be put on [[Shalai and Hallar]] and they will continue to deal damage and get counters for infinite damage.  If you have already given [[Shalai and Hallar]] Lifelink and you have a way to apply a +1/+1 counter on a creature with cards like [[Katilda, Dawnhart Prime]], [[Shalai, Voice of Plenty]] you will not need to move to combat. 

3: With [[Shalai and Hallar]] and [[Emiel the Blessed]] on the battlefield cast [[Dockside Extortionist]]. The Dockside count will have to be 4. 3 to activate Emiel's flicker effect and 1 to use her 2nd ability. This ability allows you to give Dockside a +1/+1 counter every time he enters the battlefield by paying [[symbol:gw]]. This will trigger [[Shalai and Hallar]] each time for infinite damage. 

4: All of these winning combos can be assembled or enhanced with the card [[Savage Summoning]]. This 1 drop instant allows you to cast the last creature needed for the combo with a +1/+1 counter on any turn with a ton of protection.  Pretty much only [[Mindbreak Trap]] can stop this win on the stack or if the other cretaure on board gets removed with the uncounterable creature on the stack.  To protect our combo creatures on board we run a lot of protection in the form of creatures.  [[Benevolent Bodyguard]], [[Giver of Runes]], [[Mother of Runes]], [[Skrelv, Defector Mite]], [[Cliffside Rescuer]], [[Shalai, Voice of Plenty]] and [[Sylvan Safekeeper]]. [[Borimir, Warden of the Tower]] provides protection for all of your creatures as well as a stax effect that doesn't allow your opponents to play free spells. If you need your opponent to cast a counter spell for free to save the table you can sac Borimir to make it possible.  [[Grand Abolisher]] and [[Ranger-Captain of Eos]] are obvious silence effects that can make winning easy.  A most recent addition has been [[Kutzil, Malamet Exemplar]].  This is a silence effect on a legendary creature and can even be tutored with [[Time of Need]].  [[Legolas's Quick Reflexes]] is another new addition that can protect our combo and untap a mana dork all for [[symbol:g]].  the fact that it has split second basically makes it impossible to interact with. 

5: Lastly this is a deck that can go to a beats plan if winning through an infinite damage combo becomes impossible.  we run a lot of big creatures that can be given a lot of +1/+1 counters.


TUTORS:

We run a lot of creature tutors in this deck since our whole game plan is creature focused. Green provides the best creature tutors in the game by far.  Cards like [[Invasion of Ikoria]], [[Finale of Devestation]], [[Eldritch Evolution]] and [[Chord of Calling]] put creatures directly on the battlefield. [[Eladamri's Call]] also puts a creature straight to hand.  [[Enlightened Tutor]] is very versatile in this deck because not only can it get you an artifact or enchantment, It can get you [[Heliod, Sun Crowned]] since he is an enchantment creature.  [[Ranger-Captain of Eos]] tutors a 1 drop creature to hand.  This wont get you a win con but can get you any of the 1 drop protection creatures to help secure a win.  With the high density of creatures we use [[Survival of the Fittest]] is really good in this deck.  Lastly [[Worldly Tutor]] is a classic option to put any creature on top of your library preferably right before you draw a card.


STAX PIECES:

We are not leaning full into Stax in this deck, but we are running plenty of pieces that can disrupt pretty much any strategy.  A lot of the Stax pieces can be found on easy to tutor creatures. [[Drannith Magistrate]] is one of the most powerful stax pieces in the format, Everyone knows this.  It ruins so many strategies.  [[Soulless Jailer]] and [[Rest in Peace]] are other options for shutting down graveyard strategies that so many meta decks rely on. The fact that our stack interaction is limited Archaic can be a house in this deck by copying counter spells.  Copying tutors is also great, or just taxing your opponents.  [[Aven Mindcensor]] is our best tutor hate in Naya.  [[Linvala, Keeper of Silence]] is a great stax effect that only hurts your opponents.  She is also great if you have to resort to beats.  [[Deafening Silence]] is great for this deck because of the high density of creatures and the fact that all of our wincons are creature based.  I am currently not on any other [[Rule of Law]] effects.  We can find other ways to slow our opponents down without slowing ourselves down.  One more piece of artifact/creature hate is [[Blind Obedience]].  We are in a treasure meta and this stops players from going for dockside combos and can help us get attacks through if our opponents have tapped creatures.  I recently added [[Dauntless Dismantler]].  Another solid stax piece that can really slow artifact ramp down and just blow rocks up on a 1/4 body.  


CARD ADVANTAGE:

With no card advantage in the command zone and no access to blue we have to dig a little deeper for card draw.  It can really deplete your life but its still worth a slot.  [[Esper Sentinel]] and [[Archivist of Oghma]] are very good card draw engines that you love to see in your opening hand.  [[The One Ring]] can draw you a ton of cards if the game moves into the midrange and to a lesser degree [[Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer]] can get you some good stuff off of the top of your opponents library. 


RAMP:

We are able to ramp very easily in this deck without relying on mana rocks.  [[Delighted Halfling]] makes pretty much all of our wincons uncounterable while making one of any color. Its the worst dork in the deck when not casting a legendary spell though. [[Birds of Paradise]] is the best mana dork of all time and [[Bloom tender]] can potentially make [[symbol:w]][[symbol:g]][[symbol:r]] depending on your board state.  [[Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer]] and [[Dockside Extortionist]] make treasures.  We are running a very basic mana rock package.  [[Chrome Mox]], [[Lotus Petal]], [[Mana Crypt]], [[Mox Diamond]] and [[SolRing]] are the most common CEDH rocks.  This a fantastic [[Gaea's Cradle]] deck because of the density of creatures. I've recently dropped [[Collector Ouphe]] and added a full compliment of mana rocks.  I've also added s few other faster cards like [[Tinder Wall]], [[Rite of Flame]] and the spirit guides. [[Elvish Spirit Guide]] and [[Simian Spirit Guide]].  I am treating this like the fast combo deck that it is.  The slower versions of the list were sometimes just a turn away from a protected win.  Adding rocks and fast mana spells has made this the "Catch them off Guard'' deck I wanted it to be.  


INTERACTION AND REMOVAL:

Since we don't have many counterspells outside of [[Red Elemental Blast]] and [[Pyroblast]] we need other ways to protect our spells from being countered. [[Veil of Summer]] is a great card for stopping all counter spells on a winning turn. [[Deflecting Swat]] can be used in so many ways including as a counterspell.  [[Allosaurus Shepherd]] protects our green spells from being countered.  [[Destiny Spinner]] protects all creature and enchantment spells from being countered.  [[Vexing Shusher]] can protect all of our spells and even our opponents spells if they need help stopping another players win attempt for the simple price of [[symbol:rg]].  [[Silence]] can be used to protect your win, or stop someone else. Some forms of spot removal are [[swords to plowshares]], [[March of Other Worldly Light]]  and [[Touch the Spirit Realm]].  Spirit Realm gets around most Silence effects as does [[Boseiju, Who Endures]].  I've recently added [[Solitude]] as well. 


OUTRO:

This is a very fun spin on the stax strategy.  To be able to play cards that ruin opposing strategies while looking for 1 card win cons is great.  The lack of card advantage in the command zone is made up for with the thick density of tutors and combo pieces.  It can get scary against blue players with no protection in hand or on board but that's when we just play patiently and wait for the opportune moment. This deck can win very fast and it can go into the late game very comfortably with stax pieces and creatures to apply pressure and gain you advantage.
